big_picture:
• OMC under-recovery narrows after fuel price hikes, LPG losses persist — 7 sources
• Govt cuts subsidised LPG cylinders under Ujjwala from 9 to 4 — 6 sources
• 13 TMC MPs, led by Sukhendu Ray and Kakoli Dastidar, meet Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari amid split buzz — 5 sources
• RBI unveils dollar-rupee swap window to draw inflows, steady rupee — 4 sources
• Stocks bounce back, oil pares gains as Iran and Israel signal a pause — 4 sources
• RBI moves Madras High Court against order to transfer Swami Satyananda’s money to Italy — 4 sources
• TMC faces parliamentary split as 20 MPs back NDA, plan letter to Speaker — 4 sources
• India records $7.1 billion current account surplus in March quarter — 4 sources
• RBI exempts FCNR(B), ECB swap positions from banks' NOP-INR limits — 3 sources
• India asks citizens to leave Iran amid fresh Israeli strikes — 3 sources

story_clusters.summary: Fuel price hikes have reduced the under-recovery for oil marketing companies (OMCs), but losses on LPG cylinders persist, with OMCs still incurring a shortfall of around Rs 700 per cylinder, according to government data. The price increases have been linked to geopolitical tensions, with Maharashtra Deputy CM Fadnavis crediting the PM for handling the situation effectively. Meanwhile, Karnataka CM D K Shivakumar has sought a report on the LPG cylinder price hike.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) introduced a dollar-rupee swap window to attract foreign exchange inflows and stabilize the rupee, exempting banks from net unhedged foreign exchange exposure norms for these transactions. Goldman Sachs noted that the RBI’s measures may limit further rupee depreciation, while state-owned enterprises’ external borrowings could exceed $15 billion through the concessional swap facility.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has introduced measures to enhance flexibility in banking operations, including exempting FCNR(B) and ECB swap positions from banks' NOP-INR limits and launching an FCNR(B) swap facility with rate flexibility. Additionally, the RBI's bulk deposit proposal aims to enable risk-based pricing by banks, while India's banks may raise $35-$40 billion through RBI's foreign currency deposit scheme, according to PNB CEO.

story_clusters.summary: India recorded a current account surplus of $7.1 billion in the March quarter, according to multiple sources. story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) maintained the repo rate at its June meeting, citing risks to inflation and growth amid geopolitical tensions. Analysts note that while the RBI paused rates, recent GDP growth exceeding expectations framed a week of policy shifts. Some reports suggest the RBI may align with broader Asian central banks in tightening monetary policy due to rising inflation risks.

articles.title: Orissa HC Directs GST Appeal Before GSTAT as Tribunal Is Now Functional
articles.summary: The Orissa High Court directed a petitioner to file a GST appeal before the Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) instead of pursuing it in court, as the tribunal is now operational. The petitioner was also required to comply with the pre-deposit requirement under Section 112(8). This ruling underscores the shift in dispute resolution for GST matters to the specialized tribunal.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that once the GSTAT became functional and appeal timelines were extended, disputes should be pursued before the statutory forum. The petitioner was directed to comply with the pre-deposit requirement under Section 112(8). The post Orissa HC Directs GST Appeal Before GSTAT as Tribunal Is Now Functional appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: New scheme incentivises trucks, buses for meeting emissions standards
articles.summary: The Union Cabinet approved a two-year scheme to incentivize truck and bus owners in the National Capital Region to switch to electric vehicles or upgrade to BS-VI compliant models. BS-VI is India's latest emissions standard with stricter pollution controls. The scheme aims to reduce harmful pollutants like carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide from commercial vehicles. The government's approval was granted on June 3.
articles.raw_summary: The Union Cabinet has approved a two-year scheme that incentivises truck and bus owners in the National Capital Region to turn electric or upgrade to BS-VI compliant vehicles. BS-VI is the latest emissions standard which includes stricter curbs on pollutants such as carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide. The government approved the scheme on June 3, [ ]

articles.title: Applications invited for e-taxi scheme
articles.summary: The Haryana government has invited applications for its e-taxi scheme. This initiative aims to promote electric vehicle adoption in the state's taxi sector. The scheme is part of broader efforts to support sustainable transportation and reduce emissions. Applications are open to eligible drivers and operators.

articles.title: Rajasthan HC Quashes Section 148 Proceedings as Notice Was Issued Against a Deceased Person
articles.summary: The High Court held that reassessment notices issued after the death of the assessee were a nullity and could not be sustained. However, the Revenue was granted liberty to proceed against the legal representatives in accordance with law. The post Rajasthan HC Quashes Section 148 Proceedings as Notice Was Issued Against a Deceased Person appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Parameters for compulsory selection of income-tax returns filed in FY 2025-26
articles.summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) has issued guidelines for the compulsory selection of income-tax returns filed in FY 2025-26 for scrutiny in FY 2026-27. The focus is on verifying issues arising from search, survey, and reassessment cases. This action aims to enhance tax compliance and address potential discrepancies identified through these proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: CBDT has identified search, survey, and reassessment cases for compulsory scrutiny in FY 2026-27. The guidelines highlight the Department’s focus on verifying issues arising from such proceedings. The post Parameters for compulsory selection of income-tax returns filed in FY 2025-26 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court examined whether its earlier ruling on diesel escalation reimbursement contained an apparent error. articles.title: Delhi HC Upholds Reassessment Notice as Time Granted for Reply is Excluddible from Limitation
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court held that the period consumed in granting time to the assessee under Section 148A(b) must be excluded while computing limitation. As reassessment proceedings were initiated within time, the Section 148 notice was held valid. articles.title: Understanding Equalisation Levy: Online Advertising & E-commerce
articles.summary: The Indian government has decided to abolish the Equalisation Levy, a 2% tax on online advertisements and e-commerce transactions, effective from August 1, 2024. The levy will no longer apply from the Assessment Year 2026-27 due to a sunset clause introduced in the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2024. This change impacts digital businesses and online platforms operating in India.
articles.raw_summary: Equalisation Levy, initially imposed on online advertisements and later extended to e-commerce transactions, ceases to apply from AY 2026-27 due to the sunset clause introduced by the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2024. The amendment ends the 2% levy on e-commerce supplies and services for consideration received on or after 1 August 2024. The post Understanding Equalisation Levy: Online Advertising   E-commerce appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Quashes Reassessment as Moratorium Under IBC Barred Fresh Tax Proceedings
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that reassessment proceedings under tax law cannot be initiated during the moratorium period declared by the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The tribunal held that fresh tax assessment during this period is invalid and quashed the assessment order. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle cases involving companies undergoing insolvency proceedings.

articles.title: Extended Limitation Held Invalid as Tax Proceedings Were Based Solely on Third Paty Data
articles.summary: A tribunal invalidated an extended limitation period for a service tax demand raised solely on third-party data (DGARM data and financial statements). The tribunal ruled that relying exclusively on such material could not justify the extended limitation period, leading to the demand and penalties being set aside. The case involved a service tax demand based on external data without sufficient evidence to sustain the claim.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal examined a service tax demand raised on the basis of DGARM data and financial statements. It held that reliance solely on such material could not sustain the extended limitation period, resulting in the demand and penalties being set aside. The post Extended Limitation Held Invalid as Tax Proceedings Were Based Solely on Third Paty Data appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Opaque Veil: National Security or confedential & Denial of Documents in GST Adjudication
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that an accused cannot be denied copies of documents forming part of the chargesheet solely because the prosecution is under the Official Secrets Act. The Court emphasized the need to balance fair trial rights with national security by allowing access to documents under strict confidentiality restrictions. This decision impacts how GST adjudication cases are handled, particularly regarding transparency and procedural fairness.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that an accused cannot be denied copies of documents forming part of the chargesheet merely because prosecution is under the Official Secrets Act. The Court balanced fair trial rights and national security by allowing access subject to strict confidentiality restrictions. articles.title: Taxation of Registered Non-Profit Organisations under Income Tax 2025
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act's Sections 334 to 338 introduce a separate taxation framework for registered non-profit organizations (NPOs) in India. Income from violations such as anonymous donations, prohibited investments, or misuse of funds will be taxed at 30%. This framework aims to regulate and tax specific income sources of NPOs, ensuring compliance with tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: Sections 334 to 338 prescribe a separate taxation framework for registered NPOs. Specified income arising from violations such as anonymous donations, prohibited investments, and misuse of funds is taxable at 30%. articles.title: RBI exempts bank swap deals from net unhedged FX exposure norm
articles.summary: Banks will benefit from new Reserve Bank of India rules. Swap deals for foreign currency funds will not count towards their overnight foreign currency market position. This change aims to help banks raise foreign currency more easily. The RBI had previously set a limit to control market volatility. This move is expected to provide relief and encourage foreign currency inflows.

articles.title: SRA could not back out of CoC-Approved Plan on Pretext of Conditional Letter of Intent (LoI): Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a Successful Resolution Applicant (SRA) cannot withdraw from a Committee of Creditors (CoC)-approved resolution plan by claiming the Letter of Intent (LoI) was conditional. The court stated that merely mentioning pending proceedings in the LoI did not make it conditional. This decision reinforces the binding nature of CoC-approved plans under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC).
articles.raw_summary: A Successful Resolution Applicant (SRA) could not avoid a CoC-approved resolution plan by claiming that the Letter of Intent (LoI) was conditional. The court observed that merely stating in the LoI that the resolution plan would be subject to the outcome of pending proceedings before the NCLT did not make the LoI conditional. The post SRA could not back out of CoC-Approved Plan on Pretext of Conditional Letter of Intent (LoI): Supreme Court appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ED Can Initiate PMLA Inquiry Without Prior FIR: Kerala HC
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court ruled that the Enforcement Directorate (ED) can initiate inquiry proceedings and issue summons under Section 50 of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) without needing a prior First Information Report (FIR) or complaint. The court examined whether a predicate FIR is necessary before the ED can act under the PMLA and held that such proceedings can proceed independently. This decision clarifies the ED's investigative powers under the PMLA.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether a predicate FIR is necessary before the ED can act under the PMLA. It held that inquiry proceedings and summons under Section 50 can be initiated even without a prior FIR or complaint. articles.title: Duplicate SIM exposes a structural flaw in UPI, Karnataka HC ruling shows
articles.summary: A Karnataka High Court ruling exposed a flaw in India's UPI and digital payment systems after a fraudster gained control of a victim's financial identity by obtaining a duplicate SIM card. Mobile numbers are critical for OTP verifications in RTGS/NEFT and UPI resets, making such frauds a systemic risk. The court's observation underscores vulnerabilities in digital payment security and the need for stronger safeguards.
articles.raw_summary: The negligent issuance of the duplicate SIM gave the fraudster control of the victim's financial identity, as mobile numbers remain the key channel for OTP verifications for RTGS/NEFT and also UPI resets. This decision impacts how financial crimes are investigated under the PMLA.

articles.title: End of Invisible Dyeing Costs: Compliance Demands Are Redefining the Economics of Textile Production
articles.summary: Textile dyeing, a water-intensive and polluting process, faces growing regulatory pressure globally due to water pollution and resource consumption. Manufacturers are re-evaluating their operations as compliance demands tighten, which could impact India's textile industry—a key sector for the country's exports and employment.
articles.raw_summary: Water pollution, resource consumption and regulatory scrutiny are converging on one of textile manufacturing's most established processes. Dyeing remains essential to fabric production, yet significant quantities of water, energy and chemicals are consumed in managing unfixed colourants. As compliance requirements tighten globally, manufacturers are reassessing how efficiency, wastewater management and competitiveness intersect.

articles.title: Disgorgement vs. Penalties: SAT Ruling on Tax Adjustments in Alpesh Vasanji Furiya v. SEBI
articles.summary: The SAT's ruling in Alpesh Vasanji Furiya v. SEBI is a significant clarification of the relationship between securities enforcement and tax law in India. By holding that income tax paid on unlawful gains cannot be offset against SEBI-ordered disgorgement, the tribunal has maintained the purity and effectiveness of disgorgement as an equitable remedy — ensuring that tax compliance cannot be deployed as a device to dilute the amount recoverable by SEBI in enforcement proceedings. The post Disgorge

articles.title: Chargesheet Documents Cannot Be Withheld from Accused Citing Official Secrets Act: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that chargesheet documents cannot be withheld from accused individuals by citing the Official Secrets Act, emphasizing the balance between national security and the right to a fair trial. The judgment ensures accused persons have access to key documents, reinforcing due process in criminal proceedings. The case involved balancing state secrecy concerns with constitutional rights, setting a precedent for future legal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court balances national security and fair trial rights, allowing accused access to chargesheet documents.

articles.title: Transfer of Going Concern under GST: Exemption, ITC and Documentation
articles.summary: The article explains that transferring a functioning business as a going concern is exempt from GST under Indian tax law. It emphasizes that the exemption applies when business continuity is maintained, not just asset transfer. The analysis covers input tax credit (ITC) and required documentation for such transactions. The piece is published by TaxGuru, a platform covering tax-related matters.
articles.raw_summary: Transfer of a live and functional business as a going concern qualifies as an exempt supply under GST. The key takeaway is that business continuity, not mere asset transfer, determines eligibility for exemption. articles.title: IBBI Suspends Insolvency Professional for 3 Years Due to Failure to Constitute CoC
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) suspended an Insolvency Professional for three years for failing to constitute the Committee of Creditors (CoC) within mandatory timelines. The IBBI ruled that delaying CoC formation based on settlement discussions or creditor reluctance is not permissible. This decision underscores the importance of adhering to the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) timelines set by the regulator.
articles.raw_summary: IBBI held that an Insolvency Professional cannot delay constitution of the Committee of Creditors based on settlement discussions or creditor reluctance. Failure to follow mandatory CIRP timelines resulted in a three-year suspension. The post IBBI Suspends Insolvency Professional for 3 Years Due to Failure to Constitute CoC appeared first on TaxGuru .

The petition argues that the mandate is unreasonable. The case challenges the government's Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM) List-II, which enforces local sourcing rules for solar cells.
articles.raw_summary: The writ petition challenges Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) orders that enforce ALMM List-II — the approved list of domestic solar-cell manufacturers — for projects commissioned on or after June 1, 2026

articles.title: No Tax Deduction: Form 15G and Form 15H Declarations
articles.summary: Resident individuals, non-corporate taxpayers, and senior citizens can avoid tax deducted at source (TDS) on certain incomes by submitting Form 15G or Form 15H if they meet eligibility conditions. Payers must report these declarations quarterly, obtain a Unique Identification Number (UIN), and retain records for seven years. The rules aim to streamline tax compliance for small taxpayers while ensuring proper documentation.
articles.raw_summary: Resident individuals, eligible non-corporate taxpayers, and senior citizens can avoid TDS on specified incomes by furnishing Form 15G or Form 15H where prescribed conditions are satisfied. The rules also require quarterly reporting, UIN allotment, and seven-year preservation of declarations by the payer. The post No Tax Deduction: Form 15G and Form 15H Declarations appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Foreign Procurement Hub Services Qualify as Import of Services: GSTAT
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) ruled that procurement services provided by a foreign group entity qualify as 'import of services' rather than intermediary services. This decision led to the rejection of a refund claim. The ruling clarifies tax obligations for cross-border procurement activities, impacting businesses engaged in such transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that procurement services provided by a foreign group entity were substantive services rendered on its own account and not intermediary services. As a result, the transaction qualified as import of services and the refund claim was rejected. articles.title: Greece to tax gains from crypto, sources say
articles.summary: Greece is set to introduce a 15% capital gains tax on cryptocurrencies. The Finance Ministry is drafting a law for parliamentary submission. The first 500 euros of gains will be tax-exempt. This move aims to integrate crypto assets into the nation's tax code. The tax will not apply to individual crypto mining operations.

articles.title: Reporting of Foreign Asset in Schedule FA of ITR by Resident Taxpayers
articles.summary: Resident and Ordinarily Resident taxpayers in India must report foreign assets and income in Schedule FA of their Income Tax Return (ITR). Failure to comply can result in penalties and tax scrutiny. This requirement is part of India's tax compliance framework to track offshore holdings and prevent tax evasion.

articles.title: Supreme Court releases draft regulations on AI use in courts, invites public feedback
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India has released draft regulations governing the use of artificial intelligence in courts. The rules permit AI for tasks like legal research, drafting, and court administration but prohibit AI-led judgments or sentencing and mandate human oversight. The draft invites public feedback before finalization. This marks a proactive step by the judiciary to integrate AI while ensuring accountability in legal processes.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court’s draft AI regulations permit AI for legal research, drafting and court administration, but mandate human oversight and prohibit AI-led judgments or sentencing.

articles.summary: The United Democratic Front (UDF) government in Kerala is preparing to leverage the state's potential in the 'blue economy'—activities related to oceans, seas, and coasts. The initiative aims to boost economic growth through sectors like fisheries, tourism, and renewable energy. The government's focus reflects a broader strategy to modernize Kerala's economy, though specific policy measures are yet to be detailed.

articles.title: Dharmapuri temple development project oustees seek fair compensation
articles.summary: Displaced individuals from a temple development project in Dharmapuri, Tamil Nadu, are demanding fair compensation from the government. The project, aimed at developing a temple, has led to the displacement of local residents who argue they are not being adequately compensated. The affected people are seeking policy intervention to ensure just rehabilitation and compensation. The issue highlights the intersection of infrastructure development, civil liberties, and welfare rights.

articles.title: Sovereignty of country paramount, not personal liberty in drugs case: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that national sovereignty takes precedence over personal liberty in cases involving narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances. The court stated that if there is a conflict between the two, the former will prevail. This ruling was made in the context of a drugs case and underscores the judiciary's stance on balancing individual rights with national security concerns.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday said if there is a conflict between the country's sovereignty and personal liberty, the former will take priority, especially in cases involving narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances.

articles.summary: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited (RVNL), a government-owned company, aims to launch the first prototype of the Vande Bharat Sleeper train by December 2026. The project is part of India's broader railway infrastructure modernization efforts. The sleeper version of the Vande Bharat train is expected to enhance long-distance travel options for passengers.

articles.title: How have crude oil prices behaved during the West Asia war?
articles.summary: The West Asia war pushed Brent crude from about $70 to a peak of $126 per barrel, raising fuel costs and inflation in India, which imports 90% of its oil. Prices have eased amid Iran-US peace talks but remain elevated, keeping pressure on growth, consumers and businesses.

articles.title: Trinamool Congress moves Calcutta HC over LoP row
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress has approached the Calcutta High Court over a dispute regarding the recognition of the Leader of the Opposition (LoP). The Speaker had recognized a rebel bloc led by expelled MLA Ritabrata Banerjee as the LoP, prompting the party to challenge this decision in court. The matter pertains to parliamentary procedure and the recognition of opposition leaders in the state assembly.

articles.title: Lawyers welcome SC’s AI rules against ‘algorithmic justice’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has released a draft framework to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in Indian courts. The proposed rules restrict AI to assistive roles, explicitly banning its use for decision-making or sentencing, while promoting its use for administrative efficiency and improving access to justice. Legal professionals have welcomed these measures as a balanced approach to innovation and judicial safeguards.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has released a draft framework for AI in courts, aiming to balance innovation with judicial safeguards. The proposed regulations emphasise AI as an assistive tool, strictly prohibiting its use for decision-making, adjudication, or sentencing, while encouraging its adoption for administrative efficiency and access to justice.

articles.title: UDF Govt’s Free KSRTC Rides to Women To Cost Rs 712–1,300 Cr a Year
articles.summary: The Kerala government, led by the United Democratic Front (UDF), has announced a policy to provide free bus rides to all women and transgender persons on KSRTC (Kerala State Road Transport Corporation) services. The scheme is estimated to cost the state exchequer between Rs 712 crore and Rs 1,300 crore annually. The facility aims to promote gender inclusivity and accessibility in public transport.

articles.title: Private capex set to broaden in FY27 as rate cuts spur investment: BoB
articles.summary: Private capital expenditure in India is expected to expand in FY27, driven by anticipated interest rate cuts. Sectors like consumer durables, logistics, retail, and capital goods are likely to lead this investment cycle. The Bank of Baroda (BoB) suggests that lower borrowing costs could spur business investments, benefiting industrial and supply chain growth. This reflects broader economic trends that may influence future government policies on infrastructure and industrial development.
articles.raw_summary: Consumer durables, logistics, retail and capital goods likely to lead the next phase of the investment cycle.

This situation in Nepal may have implications for regional stability and India's diplomatic engagements with its neighbors.

articles.title: Solar panel recycling yet to close cost gap before waste surge
articles.summary: The article highlights that solar panel recycling in India is not economically viable without mandatory extended producer responsibility (EPR), as the cost of recycling crystalline silicon modules—comprising 95% of installed solar panels—cannot compete with landfill disposal. Without regulatory intervention, this could lead to a waste management crisis as solar installations grow. The issue underscores the need for policy measures to address end-of-life solar panel disposal sustainably.
articles.raw_summary: Without mandatory extended producer responsibility, the economics of recycling crystalline silicon modules – which make up roughly 95% of the global installed base – cannot justify the cost gap over landfill on material recovery alone.

articles.title: Central bank turns piper to draw in foreign capital; leaves repo rate at 5.25, keeps stance neutral
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has introduced measures to boost foreign currency inflows, including incentives for external commercial borrowings and bearing hedging costs on FCNR(B) deposits. These steps aim to strengthen external buffers and support the rupee amidst market outflows. The central bank also maintained its policy rate at 5.25% and a neutral stance, while revising inflation and growth forecasts.

The central bank also maintained its policy rate at 5.25% and a neutral stance, while revising inflation and growth forecasts.

articles.title: CSR Expenditure Not Automatically Bar Section 80G Deduction: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad ruled that donations made as part of Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) obligations do not automatically disqualify companies from claiming tax deductions under Section 80G. The decision clarifies that such deductions are permissible if the conditions specified under Section 80G are met, regardless of the CSR nature of the payment. The ruling impacts how businesses account for CSR expenditures in their tax filings.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal examined whether donations made as part of CSR obligations could qualify for deduction under Section 80G. It held that deduction cannot be denied merely because the payment was made towards CSR, subject to fulfillment of Section 80G conditions. The post CSR Expenditure Not Automatically Bar Section 80G Deduction: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: India, US trade pact can be finalised after conclusion of Sec 301 investigations: Official
articles.summary: India and the US are nearing the completion of their interim trade agreement's first phase. This crucial step hinges on the conclusion of America's Section 301 investigations. Officials indicate the pact could be finalized by mid-July. Discussions are ongoing, with a high-level US team expected to visit India soon. This development is significant for bilateral trade relations.

articles.title: FTA talks with EAEU likely to take up agri, marine goods
articles.summary: India will discuss easing export compliance for marine goods, agricultural items, and processed foods with the Eurasian Economic Union. articles.title: ITC not deniable for retrospective cancellation of seller’s GST Registration: Madras HC
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that a purchasing dealer can still claim Input Tax Credit (ITC) even if the seller's GST registration is cancelled retrospectively, provided the registration was valid at the time of purchase. The court held that denying ITC solely due to retrospective cancellation would be unjust. This decision impacts businesses relying on GST compliance for tax credits and clarifies the legal stance on retrospective GST cancellations.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that a purchasing dealer can rely on a sellers valid registration at the time of purchase. ITC cannot be denied merely because the sellers registration was cancelled retrospectively. The post ITC not deniable for retrospective cancellation of seller’s GST Registration: Madras HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: New income tax act 2025 bring new relatives, whose Gifts are tax free?
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act, 2025 has been amended to include maternal and paternal grandparents and other lineal ascendants and descendants in the definition of 'relative.' This change ensures that gifts received from these relatives are now clearly exempt from income tax. The amendment provides clarity on tax-free gifts, particularly from maternal grandparents, under the new law.
articles.raw_summary: The Income Tax Act, 2025 expressly includes maternal as well as paternal lineal ascendants and descendants in the definition of relative. The key takeaway is that gifts from maternal grandparents and similar relatives now clearly qualify for tax exemption. The post New income tax act 2025 bring new relatives, whose Gifts are tax free? appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: TMC vs TMC: 59 rebel MLAs back suspended Ritabrata Banerjee as legislature party leader, party dissolves all panels
articles.summary: A faction of 59 MLAs from the Trinamool Congress (TMC) in West Bengal has backed suspended leader Ritabrata Banerjee as the legislature party leader, defying the party's decision. The TMC has dissolved all legislative panels in response. The dispute involves the anti-defection law, which requires two-thirds support (54 MLAs) to avoid disqualification for a breakaway faction.
articles.raw_summary: Under the anti-defection law, a breakaway faction requires the support of at least two-thirds of a legislature party to avoid disqualification. With the TMC having 80 MLAs in the Assembly, the threshold stands at 54.

articles.title: Govt cuts subsidised LPG cylinders to Ujjwala beneficiaries to 4
articles.summary: The government has reduced the number of subsidised LPG cylinders provided to beneficiaries of the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) from nine to four per year. PMUY, launched in May 2016, initially offered 12 subsidised cylinders annually to adult women from poor households. This policy change affects millions of households relying on affordable cooking fuel.
articles.raw_summary: Under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY), launched in May 2016 to provide deposit-free LPG connections to adult women from poor households, beneficiaries were initially entitled to 12 subsidised 14.2-kg cylinders a year. The subsidised quota was reduced to nine cylinders last year and has now been cut further to four.

The Ordinance reflects a policy decision to stimulate capital inflows into government bonds.

articles.title: Paying tax won't legitimise cash stashes
articles.summary: The Income Tax department's benami wing can now confiscate undeclared cash stashes under the Benami Act, even if taxes are paid, as per a recent tribunal ruling. The ruling clarifies that paying tax does not legitimize unaccounted wealth if the source remains undisclosed. This impacts individuals trying to regularize unaccounted money by imposing stricter enforcement of benami property laws.
articles.raw_summary: The taxman is tightening its grip on undeclared cash, with a recent tribunal ruling allowing the benami wing of the Income Tax department to confiscate it. Even if tax is paid, individuals can't escape benami law if they fail to reveal the source of the money, impacting those trying to regularize unaccounted wealth.

articles.title: Madras HC Sets Aside ITC Denial as Retrospective Cancellation of Vendor Registration Cannot Affect Purchaser
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that the retrospective cancellation of a vendor's GST registration cannot deny Input Tax Credit (ITC) to a purchaser who relied on a valid registration at the time of the transaction. The court held that ITC denial in such cases is unjustified, emphasizing the purchaser's entitlement to rely on existing valid registrations during transactions. This decision impacts tax compliance and ITC claims under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that ITC cannot be denied merely because the sellers registration was cancelled retrospectively. A purchaser is entitled to rely on a valid registration existing at the time of transaction. The report, Scratching the Surface: Victim-Blaming and Bias in Family Court Judgments, by the nonprofit organisation Right to Equality, will be shared with MPs on Tuesday at an event in parliament. articles.title: The move into gold by other nations has happened before. It’s bad news for US dollar
articles.summary: Central banks worldwide are reducing their holdings of U.S. assets in favor of gold, a trend last seen in 1971 when nations moved away from dollars before the U.S. suspended gold convertibility. The shift reflects concerns about the dollar's stability and could impact global financial systems. For India, this may influence foreign exchange reserves, trade strategies, and economic policies as the country navigates a changing monetary landscape.
articles.raw_summary: The recent shift from U.S. assets to gold mirrors 1971, when central banks dumped dollars before President Nixon ended gold convertibility.

articles.title: Homebuyers as Financial Creditor: Genuine Homebuyer V. Speculative Investor
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that individuals investing in real estate for financial returns rather than home ownership cannot be classified as financial creditors under Section 7 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). The court emphasized that insolvency proceedings are not intended for debt recovery or investment disputes. This decision clarifies the distinction between genuine homebuyers and speculative investors in the context of IBC applications.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that individuals investing for financial returns rather than home ownership cannot invoke Section 7 of the IBC. Speculative Investor appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Taxability on Transfer of Assets by HUF to its Members – A Study of Partition, Family Arrangements and Gifts
articles.summary: The article explains that transfers of assets by a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) to its members during a partition or family arrangement are not considered taxable transfers under the Income-tax Act. This means capital gains tax or gift tax generally does not apply in such cases. The analysis covers legal interpretations and implications for HUF members and tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: Distribution of assets on total or partial partition of an HUF is not regarded as a transfer under the Income-tax Act. The key takeaway is that neither capital gains tax nor gift tax generally arises on such partition. The post Taxability on Transfer of Assets by HUF to its Members – A Study of Partition, Family Arrangements and Gifts appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Arrest Illegal if Police Failed to Record Accused-Specific Reasons: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that an arrest was illegal because the police failed to record specific reasons for detaining the accused, violating Section 35 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS). The court ordered the petitioner's release on bail. This ruling underscores the need for police to provide case-specific justifications for arrests, reinforcing protections against arbitrary detention.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that the arrest violated Section 35 of the BNSS because the reasons recorded were generic and not specific to the accused. The petitioner was ordered to be released on bail. The post Arrest Illegal if Police Failed to Record Accused-Specific Reasons: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that dock identification of an accused in court—without a prior Test Identification Parade (TIP)—does not automatically weaken the prosecution's case if the accused was clearly described in the complaint or arrested immediately after the crime. The bench of Justices Pankaj Mithal and Prasanna B. Varale upheld the conviction of two individuals for kidnapping with ransom demands, setting a legal precedent for future cases involving identification procedures.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has observed that dock identification of the accused in the absence of the Test Identification Parade (TIP) would not be fatal to the prosecution's case if the accused was sufficiently described in the complaint or was arrested on the spot immediately after the occurrence of the crime. articles.title: Granting Bail In Rape Cases At Very Inception Of Trial Has 'Chilling Effect' On Safety Of Women: J&K&L High Court
articles.summary: The High Court of Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh has held that granting bail in rape cases at the very inception of trial has a chilling effect on the safety of women and that courts must remain mindful of the gravity and societal impact of such offences while considering bail applications. Setting aside a bail order passed by a trial court in a rape case, the Court observed that the trial court had relied upon irrelevant considerations and had failed to account for the seriousness of the allegation

articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that the term 'Establishment' under the Census Act should not be narrowly interpreted, allowing private sector employees—including those from LIC—to be requisitioned as enumerators for census work. Once appointed, these individuals are deemed public servants and must fulfill their statutory duties. The decision clarifies the scope of the Census Act and its application to private sector employees.

articles.title: SC’s expanding definition of ‘family’—how married daughters won, one judgement at a time
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that marital status should not determine dependency or financial need in legal definitions of 'family'. This decision strengthens the rights of married daughters by removing discriminatory barriers in inheritance and welfare considerations. The ruling builds on previous judgments, progressively expanding family definitions to ensure gender justice. The Court emphasized that such distinctions lack rational basis, reinforcing constitutional principles of equality.
articles.raw_summary: In latest ruling Tuesday, Supreme Court noted that ‘marital status bears no rational nexus’ to considerations like dependency or financial need.

articles.title: From Australia to Europe, countries move to curb children's social media access
articles.summary: Several countries, including Australia, Britain, and France, are introducing strict rules to limit social media access for children under 16. These measures aim to protect young users from online harms like cyberbullying and exposure to harmful content. Tech companies are now required to implement age verification and stricter content moderation. The trend reflects growing global concern over the impact of social media on children's mental health and safety.

Britain, France, and others are considering similar age restrictions. These measures aim to protect young users from online harms. Tech firms face new demands for age verification and content moderation.

The decision aims to strengthen the state's paddy processing sector and is effective immediately.

It is expected to strengthen the state's paddy processing.

articles.title: Meta alert helps UP cops save 25-year-old from taking his own life
articles.summary: A 25-year-old man in Uttar Pradesh was prevented from taking his own life after a Meta alert (likely from Facebook or WhatsApp) notified local police. The incident underscores the role of social media platforms in identifying and intervening in mental health crises. Authorities have not yet indicated any policy response, but the case may prompt discussions on digital platforms' responsibilities in crisis situations.
articles.raw_summary: Meta alert helps UP cops save 25-year-old from taking his own life

articles.title: South America to ramp up India outreach despite geography
articles.summary: In an era of growing diplomatic relations, South American countries are increasingly turning to India, with Argentina leading the charge by acknowledging the strategic and economic partnerships at play. As India's sixth largest trading ally, Argentina plays a vital role by providing edible oils that enhance India's food security posture.

Krishnan described his time as a privilege, working at the core of US government AI policy development.

articles.title: Processing mechanisms key to food security in South Asia
articles.summary: An editorial argues that food processing is crucial for South Asia's food security, linking it to economic and nutritional outcomes. It suggests that processing should be treated as a development priority, not just an industrial concern. The piece underscores the interconnectedness of food loss, income generation, and nutrition in shaping policy responses.

articles.title: No mobile internet in parts of Jaipur today ahead of JDA encroachment drive
articles.summary: The Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) has suspended mobile internet, social media, and bulk messaging services in parts of Jaipur until June 9 ahead of an anti-encroachment drive. The suspension is justified by concerns over potential law and order issues and misinformation. The move affects communication and digital access for residents in the affected areas.

articles.title: NCP’s Vikram Kakade says ₹10 crore to Jay Pawar was plot advance, not loan; declares assets of over ₹150 crore
articles.summary: Newly elected Maharashtra Legislative Council member Vikram Kakade has clarified that the ₹10 crore listed against Jay Pawar in his election affidavit was an advance payment for a land transaction and not a loan. The Pune-based builder, who was elected unopposed from the Pune local authorities constituency, disclosed family assets worth more than ₹150 crore, including luxury vehicles, investments, and real estate holdings in India and Dubai.

articles.title: Adani Energy Solutions vs Power Grid Corp: Which stock is Jefferies betting on?
articles.summary: Jefferies, a brokerage firm, has expressed a bullish outlook on both Adani Energy Solutions (AESL) and Power Grid Corporation of India. The firm sees stronger growth potential in AESL due to its transmission expansion and smart metering initiatives, expecting its earnings to outpace Power Grid. Improving valuations and execution momentum could lead to a potential re-rating of AESL's stock.
articles.raw_summary: Jefferies remains bullish on both Adani Energy Solutions and Power Grid Corp but sees significantly stronger growth in AESL, driven by transmission expansion and smart metering. articles.title: China's global ecommerce push stalls as Iran war lifts costs, dampens demand
articles.summary: China's low-cost e-commerce model, which relies on shipping cheap goods globally, is facing challenges due to rising costs from the Middle East conflict and existing U.S. tariffs. Logistics companies like DHL Express have imposed fuel surcharges, further straining the business model. The situation reflects broader trade disruptions that could impact global supply chains and e-commerce dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: The business models, based on flying $5 dresses from Chinese factories to shoppers around the world, were already under pressure after U.S. President Donald Trump introduced tariffs and axed customs waivers on low-value parcels last year. Soaring logistics costs stemming ‌from the Middle East ⁠conflict are adding ⁠to the strain, data shows and industry insiders say, with shippers like DHL Express imposing hefty fuel surcharges.

articles.title: India's cybersecurity talent gap grows as skills prove hard to find
articles.summary: India faces a critical cybersecurity talent shortage, with nearly 39,000 unfilled positions, especially in emerging fields like IoT and blockchain. High attrition rates and increasing cyberattacks exacerbate the issue. New regulations demand greater compliance, while AI alone cannot bridge the gap, highlighting the need for skilled professionals and educational reforms.

High attrition rates and increasing cyberattacks exacerbate the issue. New regulations demand greater compliance, while AI alone cannot bridge the gap, highlighting the need for skilled professionals and educational reforms.

articles.title: Here to win quick commerce, will become market leader in near-term: Samir Kumar, Amazon India chief
articles.summary: Amazon is planning to aggressively expand its 10-minute delivery service, Amazon Now, aiming for market leadership despite moderating quick commerce growth. The ecommerce giant plans to launch in over 100 cities with 1,000 dark stores, emphasising customer experience, wide selection, and speed to compete with rivals like Blinkit and Zepto.

articles.title: Rupee slide pushes overseas students back to lenders for top-up loans
articles.summary: A significant rupee depreciation has forced Indian students studying abroad to seek top-up loans from lenders, as initial loan amounts fall short of rising tuition and living expenses. This has led to a nearly threefold increase in demand for additional funding, with students requiring Rs 1-6 lakh more.

articles.title: Exports rise 15 per cent during April-May, government official says
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports show strong growth. Exports rose by 15 percent in April-May 2026-27. This happened despite global economic uncertainties. Petroleum products drove April's outbound shipments. This was the highest in over four years. However, the trade deficit widened. Imports also saw an uptick. The Commerce Ministry will release May's trade data soon.

articles.title: 'Last year we exported Rs 35,000 crore components to China': Ashwini Vaishnaw
articles.summary: India's electronics manufacturing is on the rise. Last year, components worth 35,000 crore rupees were exported to China. Around 250 new component manufacturing factories are expected in the next two to three years. This expansion is creating 25 lakh quality jobs. India is moving up the value chain, designing and manufacturing complex products.

articles.summary: Today, India is the world’s largest producer of milk, pulses, and spices; the largest exporter of rice; and among the leading producers of wheat, cotton, sugarcane, fruits, vegetables, eggs, and fish.

articles.title: Kashmir-Ladakh all-weather link ‘Zojila Tunnel’ nears reality; Gadkari to trigger breakthrough blast
articles.summary: The Zojila Tunnel, a key all-weather road link between Kashmir and Ladakh, is nearing completion with Union Minister Nitin Gadkari set to trigger a breakthrough blast. The project, part of the government's infrastructure push, aims to provide year-round connectivity to the strategically important region. The tunnel is being developed by the National Highways and Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (NHIDCL) under the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways.

articles.title: Inflation poses bigger risk than growth this fiscal as monsoon and crude weigh on rural demand: Nuvama
articles.summary: In FY27, India's economy grapples with inflation that hits hardest in rural regions, where rising food prices become an urgent concern. The eyes of policymakers are fixed on the monsoon's trajectory, a key determinant for crop health. Global factors, such as elevated crude oil prices and geopolitical strife, further exacerbate inflationary trends.

articles.title: India inflation likely rose to 4% in May as food, fuel costs climb
articles.summary: India's inflation likely climbed to the Reserve Bank of India's 4% target in May, driven by rising vegetable prices and increased fuel costs. This trend marks a shift from the previous 15 months of below-target inflation, with economists anticipating further upward pressure due to elevated wholesale prices and potential monsoon impacts.

articles.title: Indians are growing more pessimistic about the economy. RBI Survey shows why
articles.summary: Urban consumers grew more pessimistic about the economy, jobs, and spending in May, with the Current Situation Index falling for the third consecutive round. Professional forecasters also lowered India's growth outlook for 2026-27 to 6.5%, citing emerging signs of softer demand and weakening sentiment.

articles.title: India can grow over 8% despite oil shocks, crude impact overblown in narrative: World Bank exec
articles.summary: India's economy shows strong growth, defying fears of oil price shocks. Neelkanth Mishra, World Bank Executive Director, states India is well-positioned to handle higher oil prices. Domestic demand remains robust, with positive indicators in car sales and mall footfalls. India's refining capabilities offer a buffer, mitigating the impact of crude price increases. Growth is projected to remain strong, near 7.5-8%.

articles.title: India’s growth faces crude and monsoon test as FY27 GDP seen moderating to 6.5%
articles.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to about 6.5 percent in fiscal year 2027. Higher costs, global tensions, and a potentially weak monsoon are key concerns. Despite these challenges, private spending and investment are expected to support growth above 6 percent. The economy finished fiscal year 2026 strongly, providing a solid foundation for the upcoming year.

articles.title: Rising fuel prices to strain household budgets as West Asia crisis pushes crude above USD 100: Chief Economist Manoranjan Sharma
articles.summary: Fuel prices are climbing globally, impacting Indian households. The West Asia conflict is driving up crude oil costs, making higher fuel prices unavoidable. India, reliant on oil imports through the Strait of Hormuz, faces significant challenges. While the government aims to protect consumers, global energy shocks present limitations.

articles.title: Make in India efforts showing results as import dependence falls in key sectors despite global shocks: Bank of Baroda
articles.summary: India's manufacturing sector is becoming more self-reliant. Import dependence is falling in electricals, chemicals, and capital goods. This trend strengthens domestic production. Policy initiatives like Make in India are driving this shift. The economy is better protected from global supply shocks. Sectors like consumer goods show lower import intensity. Some sectors remain import-dependent and need monitoring.

articles.title: India’s rising export champion faces a critical credibility test
articles.summary: India's seafood exports are soaring, reaching record highs and aiming for $30 billion within five years. This quality issue risks undermining the hard-won international trust crucial for sustained growth and premium market access.

articles.title: Malviya Nagar fire incident: MEA extends assistance, in touch with embassies
articles.summary: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a massive fire engulfed a B B hotel in Delhi's Malviya Nagar on Wednesday. Firefighters rescued 37 occupants, while 39 victims were taken to Max Hospital, where 18 were declared dead and 15 remain in intensive care. Authorities said the hotel lacked a mandatory fire NOC, and police have launched a manhunt for the owner while investigating possible safety violations. Foreign nationals accounted for 17 of the 21 fatalities. Prime Mini

articles.title: Tigress Zeenat gives birth to 4 cubs in Odisha's Similipal, boosting tiger conservation
articles.summary: Tigress Zeenat, relocated from Maharashtra's Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve to Odisha's Similipal Tiger Reserve, has given birth to four healthy cubs. Conservationists note that the cubs' high genetic diversity could strengthen Similipal's tiger population. This event is seen as a boost to India's tiger conservation efforts under existing wildlife protection policies.
articles.raw_summary: In a major boost to tiger conservation efforts, tigress Zeenat—translocated from Maharashtra's Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve to Odisha's Similipal Tiger Reserve—has given birth to four healthy cubs. Conservationists say the cubs' high genetic diversity represents a significant step toward strengthening Similipal's tiger population and securing the future of the reserve's big cats.

articles.title: Donald Trump, Bernie Sanders and Sam Altman are all talking about public ownership in AI
articles.summary: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, met with US presidential candidates Bernie Sanders and Donald Trump to discuss the idea of public ownership in AI development. These debates highlight growing concerns about AI governance and could shape future policy decisions in the United States and beyond.
articles.raw_summary: Sam Altman meets Bernie Sanders and Donald Trump as public ownership of AI, data center backlash, and bipartisan regulation debates reshape US tech policy.

articles.title: Anthropic says AI could build its own successor, calls for slowdown in development
